sources.title: Should you trust AI to manage your money? The finance industry is betting you will
claims: Robinhood Strategies has 250,000 subscribers paying on average $250/yr
33% of consumers across all age groups are using ChatGPT/Claude for investment guidance
Fidelity Freya enables natural-language financial planning ('I'm 34, just had a baby, $80K 401k...')

sources.title: Robo Advisory Market Size & Share | Industry Report, 2030
claims: Robo advisory services market expected to reach USD 14.29 billion in 2025
CAGR of 30.80% from 2025 to 2030
Market projected to reach $54.73 billion by 2030

sources.title: Robo-advisory Services Market Size & Share Analysis
claims: Robo advisory market projected to reach USD 14.29 billion in 2025
Growing at a CAGR of 30.80% from 2025 to 2030

sources.title: AI Robo-Advisors vs Human Financial Advisors: The $3.2 Trillion Market That Will Transform Personal Wealth by 2033
claims: Robo-advisors managed approximately $1.4 trillion AUM at end of 2025
Industry projections place AUM at $3.2 trillion by 2033
LLM-powered advisors handle far more complex scenarios than questionnaire-based predecessors

sources.title: The largest Robo-Advisors by AUM in 2026
claims: Vanguard Digital Advisor: $311B AUM (29% market share)
Schwab Intelligent Portfolios: ~24% market share
Betterment: $45.9B AUM
Wealthfront: $42.9B AUM ($41.7B discretionary)

sources.title: GenAI: Continuing and Emerging Trends — 2026 Annual Regulatory Oversight Report
claims: FINRA introduced a dedicated AI governance section in its 2026 Oversight Report
Covers governance, recordkeeping, supervision, and autonomous agent risks
Firms expected to treat AI tools as high-risk vendors with documented due diligence

sources.title: Robo Advisory Market Size, Share, Trends | Growth Report, 2034
claims: Robo Advisory Market projected to grow from USD 14.08 billion in 2026 to USD 102.03 billion by 2034
CAGR of 28.10% during the forecast period
Hybrid robo-advisors segment to account for 56.53% of total market share in 2026

sources.title: History of Robo-Advisors and Automated Investing
claims: Betterment and Wealthfront both launched in 2008 as first robo-advisors
From 2008 to 2015, 131 additional robo-advisor firms were launched
Robo-advisors managed $870 billion in assets in 2022

sources.title: AI Compliance for Firms and RIAs in 2026
claims: 40% of investment adviser firms have implemented AI tools internally
44% of those firms have no formal testing or validation of AI outputs
SEC's 2026 Examination Priorities identified 'Emerging Financial Technology' as key risk

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence: U.S. Securities and Commodities Guidelines for Responsible Use
claims: SEC applies the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 to AI use — no AI-specific rules yet
SEC took action against firms for 'AI washing' under the Marketing Rule
EU DORA now requires AI operational resilience assessments for European financial services

sources.title: SEC Sets 2026 Exam Focus on AI Rules and Compliance
claims: SEC's 2026 examination priorities explicitly target AI and emerging financial technology
Firms must document AI governance, testing, and vendor oversight to pass examination

sources.title: FINRA's 2026 Regulatory Oversight Report: Continued Focus on Generative AI and Emerging Agent-Based Risks
claims: FINRA's 2026 report includes dedicated section on generative AI for the first time
Report addresses agentic AI and autonomous decision-making in financial advice

notes: All market size figures vary between research publishers (Fortune Business Insights, Grand View Research, Mordor Intelligence) due to different scope definitions (robo-advisory software market vs. AUM-based sizing). growth_5y estimate (+320%) based on robo-advisory market revenue from ~$4B in 2021 to ~$16.79B in 2026 — treat as directional. growth_1y (+31%) uses CAGR from Grand View Research. growth_3m (+8%) is a quarterly approximation [unverified] — use with caution in any published report.
